sanskrit:
यस्य सर्वे समारम्भाः कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः।
ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः॥
transliteration:
yasya sarve samārambhāḥ kāma-saṅkalpa-varjitāḥ
jñānāgni-dagdha-karmāṇaṁ tam āhuḥ paṇḍitaṁ budhāḥ
literal_meaning: One whose undertakings are all devoid of desire and self-centered motive, whose actions are burnt in the fire of knowledge—him the wise call a sage.
contextual_meaning: True maturity is acting without emotional dependency on outcomes; the moment personal craving is seen through, the psyche stops accumulating stress (karma) and lives in uncluttered presence.
psychological_state: Post-egoic functioning: behavior flows from values, not from compensatory wants.
trigger_scenario: You finish a demanding project at work, receive praise, yet feel oddly empty—realizing the chase for recognition was the only thing fueling you.
anchor_text: Act without craving, let knowledge burn self-interest, and live free from stress.
